source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure of a newly launched shoe rack from a Taobao store, it can be abstracted into a diagram where $l_1\parallel l_2\parallel l_3$. Lines $AC$ and $DF$ are intersected by $l_1$, $l_2$, $l_3$. If $AB=30\,\text{cm}$, $BC=50\,\text{cm}$, and $EF=40\,\text{cm}$, what is the length of $DE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
Solution: Since $l_1 \parallel l_2 \parallel l_3$,\\
therefore $\frac{AB}{BC}=\frac{DE}{EF}$\\
Therefore $\frac{30}{50}=\frac{DE}{40}$\\
Solving, we find that $DE=\boxed{24}$.

requires setting up a proportion from AB, BC, and EF before solving for DE
this is not direct lookup; it involves at least theorem selection plus arithmetic computation
verifiability: target answer is a single numeric value ('$DE=24$'), which is easy to check
the reasoning can be independently verified by the standard proportional segments theorem
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support usability, though manifest inconsistencies slightly reduce confidence
